extra.reasoning: This is a state machine. The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent), i.e., no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true. Transition t5 [on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks>=8): sent := true] directly sets sent=true while opted_out is true, breaking the invariant. Counterexample execution (10 events, within the 12-event bound): Start at opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0. Eight 'tick' events drive ticks 0->1->...->8 (t4 also sets sent=false each time). Then 'optout' (t1) sets opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks remains 8. Then 'send_attempt': guard of t5 (opted_out and ticks>=8) holds, so t5 fires and sets sent=true. The resulting state is opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=8, which violates opted_out implies not sent. (Although t3, send_attempt if opted_out, would set sent=false, t5 is also enabled and its firing produces a reachable violating state; since the property must hold on every reachable execution, the existence of this execution falsifies it.)
